label: thesis_v1_uncalibrated_parameter_contract

heating_model.recoil_energy_convention: complete absorption-spontaneous-emission cycle deposits 2 E_rec per primary or reabsorbed scattering event
heating_model.reference: Gajdacz et al., Rev. Sci. Instrum. 84, 083105 (2013)

canonical_accumulated_snr.purpose: Fig. 3.2 and its supporting thesis table must use this same parameter set
canonical_accumulated_snr.quantum_efficiency_status: experimentally plausible dissertation screening value; not measured
canonical_accumulated_snr.read_noise_status: experimentally plausible dissertation screening value; not measured
canonical_accumulated_snr.observable: analytical peak object-space camera pixel before NA/PSF blur
canonical_accumulated_snr.normalisation: absolute dimensionless SNR; no spatial summation

canonical_route_a.implementation_rule: reference group P=1.0 mW and tau=90 us; equivalent settings use tau_us=90/P_mW after checking camera timing and saturation
canonical_route_a.quantitative_model: heating plus reabsorption with strict integer accepted-frame stopping

legacy_table_5_1_audit.observable: ideal analytical peak object-space camera pixel
legacy_table_5_1_audit.status: deprecated because SNR and N_max use different exposure times

legacy_budget_label_audit.status: 52/25/24 are 40 us results and must not be labelled 15 us

reporting_rules: Every numerical conclusion must name its parameter-set identifier.
Every SNR must state spatial observable, QE, read-noise model, and whether NA/PSF is included.
Every frame count must state continuous budget or strict integer accepted frames.
Every destruction result must state clean loss or heating plus reabsorption.
GHz detunings in thesis prose and tables must be labelled |Delta|/(2 pi).
Legacy mixed-parameter values must not be reused as thesis results.
